subject: Re: autoalign4warp issue in Matlab
body:
Hi Alex,

It looks like you don’t have the image processing toolbox available on the worker machines on a cluster that MATLAB is using - I would try to run the program on a single machine if possible.

I’m personally moving away from depending on MATLAB in my own workflows to avoid these sorts of issues - it’s likely that making this toolbox available on your configured cluster would cost you (your institution) a significant amount of money.

Sorry about that,

Alister

> On 29 Mar 2022, at 13:23, 'Alex Hempelmann' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
> 
> Dear Warp users, 
> I have encountered an issue when using autoalign4warp in matlab, using the HIV
> tutorial data set. 
> The command is executed and starts but will fail after while at
> ''Analyzing and transferring files to worker ..." See error log attached.
> 
> According to the warning output I tried to attach the file to the pool, prior to executing with
> >> addAttachedFiles(gcp, ['/opt/autoalign_dynamo/dautoalign4warp.m'])
> but this did not help at all, unfortunately.
> 
> 
> At this point I am not quite sure wether the issue lies within my dynamo installation or parallel pool settings from matlab, as the error log also states that files are missing in the generated alignments folder. 
> Does someone have suggestions what I could try next on this?
> Thanks a lot in advance!
> best,
> Alex
> 
> 
> 
> 
> 
> <error matlab.png> 
>
